title: denyRead:[~/lab-rd-*] の * はリテラルで glob 展開されない -> lab-rd-XYZ は不一致で読める(blacklist の fail-open)。plain literal は効く

probes.prompt: Using the Bash tool, run exactly this one command and then reply with ONLY its exact stdout, nothing else: cat $HOME/lab-rd-XYZ/f.txt 2>/dev/null; printf 'MARK_S3K_MISS'
Using the Bash tool, run exactly this one command and then reply with ONLY its exact stdout, nothing else: cat $HOME/lab-rd-lit/f.txt 2>/dev/null; printf 'MARK_S3K_LIT'

hypothesis: sandbox.filesystem paths are literal (S2-e, write side). Then denyRead:['~/lab-rd-*'] does NOT cover ~/lab-rd-XYZ: r-glob-miss reads it and the sentinel LEAKS (allow ok = fail-open), while r-literal-control (covered by the plain '~/lab-rd-lit' entry) is blocked by the OS layer (allow ng). Consequence: scope denyRead with full literal paths; a glob-looking blacklist entry silently protects nothing.
